Double-precision extended-precision exponential function (__gf_dpexexp) with exponential constants.

### 7. andes_dpexlog.s
Double-precision extended-precision logarithm function (__gf_dpexlog) with logarithm tables and constants.

### 8. andes_dpexsqrt.s
Double-precision extended-precision square root function (__gf_dpexsqrt) using Newton-Raphson iteration.

### 9. andes_dpreduct.s
Double-precision argument reduction function (__gf_dpreduct) for trigonometric range reduction.

### 10. andes_dpsincon.s
Double-precision sine constants table (__gf_dpsincon).

### 11. andes_exadd.s
Extended-precision addition helper (__gf_exadd) for extended-precision arithmetic.

### 12. andes_exdiv.s
Extended-precision division helper (__gf_exdiv) with multi-step division logic.

### 13. andes_exmul.s
Extended-precision multiplication helper (__gf_exmul) for 64-bit multiplication.

### 14. andes_exp.s
Exponent extraction function (andes_exp) counting leading zeros.

### 15. andes_exsub.s
Extended-precision subtraction helper (__gf_exsub) with normalization.

### 16. andes_fpcoscof.s
Single-precision cosine coefficients table (__gf_fpcoscof).

### 17. andes_fpexatan.s
Single-precision extended-precision arctangent function (__gf_fpexatan) with table lookups.

### 18. andes_fpexexp.s
Single-precision extended-precision exponential function (__gf_fpexexp) with polynomial evaluation.

### 19. andes_fpexlog.s
Single-precision extended-precision logarithm function (__gf_fpexlog) with normalized value handling.

### 20. andes_fpexsqrt.s
Single-precision extended-precision square root function (__gf_fpexsqrt) using reciprocal square root.

### 21. andes_fpreduct.s
Single-precision argument reduction function (__gf_fpreduct).

### 22. andes_fpsincof.s
Single-precision sine coefficients table (__gf_fpsincof).

### 23. andes_n.s
Helper functions qn and pn for Bessel function computation with polynomial chains.

### 24. andes_p4.s
Degree-4 polynomial evaluator (andes_p4) wrapper.

### 25. andes_p4ac.s
Degree-4 polynomial evaluator with addition (andes_p4ac).

Degree-5 polynomial evaluator (andes_p5) wrapper.

Degree-5 polynomial evaluator with addition (andes_p5ac).

Degree-8 polynomial evaluator with addition (andes_p8ac).

Rational approximation function (andes_r) using Bessel P/Q coefficients with polynomial chains.

Multiple rational approximation variants (andes_r0, andes_r1, andes_r2, andes_r3, andes_r4) using different coefficient tables (RSA, RSB, PQA, PQ) for exp/sinh/cosh family functions.

input.description: Survey remaining andes helpers for size
input.prompt:
Working dir: /home/nick/work/picolibc_agentic/libm/machine/riscv/

Read and summarize the structure of these RISC-V assembly files (do NOT skim - read the full content and identify patterns). For each, report: total line count, whether there are repeated/boilerplate blocks, any obvious size-reducing opportunities, and whether the function just calls helpers vs doing bulk work:

1. andes_dpreduct.s
2. andes_fpreduct.s
3. andes_dpexexp.s
4. andes_dpexlog.s
5. andes_dpexsqrt.s
6. andes_dpexatan.s
7. andes_fpexexp.s
8. andes_fpexlog.s
9. andes_fpexsqrt.s
10. andes_fpexatan.s
11. andes_r.s
12. andes_r4.s
13. andes_n.s
14. andes_0.s
15. andes_1.s

Specifically look for (these patterns we have successfully optimized before):
- `li` + `sw` pairs that could use `sw x0` (store zero)
- Duplicate `li` constants that could be hoisted
- Back-to-back `mv` instructions forming a 3-way swap via a temp that could be simplified
- Dead code (results computed but never used)
- Consecutive `lw`/`sw` sequences that could become batched
- Redundant save/restore of callee-saved registers not actually clobbered
- `tail` to a nearby function where inlining would reduce code (polynomial evaluator chains)
- Argument-swap patterns before calling __adddf3/__subdf3/__muldf3
- Repeated identical instruction sequences that could be factored into a small helper

Also report the file sizes in bytes (ls -l) for each .s file. And run: `ls -la test/*_opt.elf 2>/dev/null | head` to see if there are built artifacts.

DO NOT suggest or write any code changes. Just identify patterns. This is an analysis-only task.

content.content:
1→# Makefile for libm accuracy test — A/B comparison
     2→# Target: RV32IM or RV64IM soft-float (Andes optimized assembly)
     3→#
     4→# Usage:
     5→#   make size-all       - report .text size for each assembly function
     6→#   make size-sinf      - report .text size for a single function
     7→#   make test-all       — build, run opt vs ref in QEMU, diff outputs
     8→#   make test-sinf      — test a single function
     9→#
    10→# Toolchain selection:
    11→#   make XLEN=32 ...    - use RV32 toolchain (default)
    12→#   make XLEN=64 ...    - use RV64 toolchain
    13→
    14→XLEN ?= 32

file.content:
# Makefile for libm accuracy test — A/B comparison
# Target: RV32IM or RV64IM soft-float (Andes optimized assembly)
#
# Usage:
#   make size-all       - report .text size for each assembly function
#   make size-sinf      - report .text size for a single function
#   make test-all       — build, run opt vs ref in QEMU, diff outputs
#   make test-sinf      — test a single function
#
# Toolchain selection:
#   make XLEN=32 ...    - use RV32 toolchain (default)
#   make XLEN=64 ...    - use RV64 toolchain

XLEN ?= 32

ifeq ($(XLEN),64)
  TOOLCHAIN  = /local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast542/build-toolchain/linux/nds64le-elf-mculib-v5
  PREFIX     = $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv64-elf-
  ARCH       = -march=rv64imc -mabi=lp64
  QEMU       = /local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64
  QEMU_FLAGS = -cpu andes-ax25 -nographic -M andes_ae350 -semihosting -bios
else
  TOOLCHAIN  = /local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast542/build-toolchain/linux/nds32le-elf-mculib-v5
  PREFIX     = $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv32-elf-
  ARCH       = -march=rv32imc -mabi=ilp32
  QEMU       = /local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv32
  QEMU_FLAGS = -cpu andes-n25 -nographic -M andes_ae350 -semihosting -bios
endif

CC  = $(PREFIX)gcc
AS  = $(PREFIX)gcc
LD  = $(PREFIX)gcc

CFLAGS   = $(ARCH) -O2 -mvh -Wall
ASFLAGS  = $(ARCH)
LDFLAGS     = $(ARCH) -mvh -lm
LDFLAGS_OPT = $(ARCH) -mvh -L. -landes

SIZE = $(PREFIX)size
NM   = $(PREFIX)nm
AR   = $(PREFIX)ar

# Andes helper assembly (shared internals — not standalone math functions)
ANDES_SRCS = $(wildcard andes_*.s)
ANDES_OBJS = $(ANDES_SRCS:.s=.o)

# Assembly sources — single-precision
ASM_SRCS = sf_atan.s sf_ceil.s sf_cos.s sf_fabs.s sf_floor.s sf_frexp.s \
           sf_ldexp.s sf_modf.s sf_sin.s sf_tan.s sf_tanh.s \
           sf_acos.s sf_asin.s sf_cosh.s sf_exp.s sf_fmod.s sf_log.s \
           sf_log10.s sf_pow.s sf_sinh.s sf_sqrt.s \
           sf_fpclassify.s
ASM_OBJS = $(ASM_SRCS:.s=.o)

# Assembly sources — double-precision
S_ASM_SRCS = s_sin.s s_cos.s s_tan.s s_atan.s s_asin.s s_acos.s s_fabs.s \
             s_ceil.s s_floor.s s_exp.s s_sinh.s s_cosh.s s_tanh.s \
             s_log.s s_log10.s s_sqrt.s s_fmod.s s_pow.s s_frexp.s \
             s_ldexp.s s_modf.s s_fpclassify.s s_expm1.s s_log1p.s \
             s_rint.s s_round.s s_trunc.s s_fmax.s s_fmin.s s_ilogb.s \
             s_nextafter.s s_remquo.s
S_ASM_OBJS = $(S_ASM_SRCS:.s=.o)

# Note: sf_sincos.c and sf_scalbn.c need picolibc headers, excluded from
# standalone size target. Measure those via full meson build.

# Single-precision functions (must have test_<name>f.c and sf_<name>.s)
FUNCS_SINGLE = sinf cosf tanf atanf asinf acosf fabsf ceilf floorf expf \
               sinhf coshf tanhf logf log10f sqrtf fmodf powf frexpf ldexpf \
               modff fpclassifyf

# Double-precision functions with s_*.s assembly (opt vs ref comparison)
FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT = sin cos tan atan asin acos fabs ceil floor exp sinh cosh \
                   tanh log log10 sqrt fmod pow frexp ldexp modf fpclassify \
                   expm1 log1p rint round trunc fmax fmin ilogb nextafter remquo

# Double-precision functions without assembly (run only)
FUNCS_DOUBLE_NOOPT = acosh atanh erf erfc lgamma \
                     hypot remainder lrint lround

# All functions to test
FUNCS = $(FUNCS_SINGLE) $(FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT) $(FUNCS_DOUBLE_NOOPT)

# Test output directory
TESTDIR = test

# Verbose build output (set V=1 to see brief build progress)
V ?= 0
ifeq ($(V),1)
  Q =
  MSG_AS  = @printf "  AS\t%s\n" $@;
  MSG_CC  = @printf "  CC\t%s\n" $@;
  MSG_LD  = @printf "  LD\t%s\n" $@;
  MSG_AR  = @printf "  AR\t%s\n" $@;
else
  Q = @
  MSG_AS =
  MSG_CC =
  MSG_LD =
  MSG_AR =
endif

libandes.a: $(ANDES_OBJS)
	$(MSG_AR)$(Q)$(AR) rcs $@ $^

# --- Pattern rules ---

%.o: %.s
	$(MSG_AS)$(Q)$(AS) $(ASFLAGS) -c -o $@ $<

$(TESTDIR)/%.o: $(TESTDIR)/%.c
	$(MSG_CC)$(Q)$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c -o $@ $<

# For single-precision functions: build opt (with asm) and ref (toolchain only) ELFs.
# The asm object name: strip trailing 'f' from func name to get sf_<base>.o
# e.g. sinf -> sf_sin.o, log10f -> sf_log10.o, fpclassifyf -> sf_fpclassify.o
define FUNC_RULES_SINGLE
$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o sf_$(patsubst %f,%,$(1)).o libandes.a
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$(filter-out libandes.a,$$^) $$(LDFLAGS_OPT)

$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$^ $$(LDFLAGS)

test-$(1): $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf
	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf < /dev/null > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt
	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf < /dev/null > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt
	@if diff -q $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt > /dev/null 2>&1; then \
		printf "  %-14s PASS\n" $(1); \
	else \
		printf "  %-14s FAIL (%d diffs)\n" $(1) \
			$$$$(diff $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt | grep -c '^[<>]'); \
	fi

size-$(1): sf_$(patsubst %f,%,$(1)).o
	@printf "  %-20s %d\n" $(1) `$$(SIZE) -A $$< | awk '/\.text/ {print $$$$2}'`
endef

# For double-precision functions with assembly: opt vs ref comparison
define FUNC_RULES_DOUBLE_OPT
$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o s_$(1).o libandes.a
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$(filter-out libandes.a,$$^) $$(LDFLAGS_OPT)

$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$^ $$(LDFLAGS)

test-$(1): $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf
	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf < /dev/null > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt
	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf < /dev/null > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt
	@if diff -q $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt > /dev/null 2>&1; then \
		printf "  %-14s PASS\n" $(1); \
	else \
		printf "  %-14s FAIL (%d diffs)\n" $(1) \
			$$$$(diff $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt | grep -c '^[<>]'); \
	fi

size-$(1): s_$(1).o
	@printf "  %-20s %d\n" $(1) `$$(SIZE) -A $$< | awk '/\.text/ {print $$$$2}'`
endef

# For double-precision functions without assembly: run only (no opt to compare)
define FUNC_RULES_DOUBLE
$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$^ $$(LDFLAGS)

test-$(1): $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf
	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf < /dev/null > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt
	@if [ -s $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt ]; then \
		printf "  %-14s PASS\n" $(1); \
	else \
		printf "  %-14s FAIL\n" $(1); \
	fi
endef

$(foreach f,$(FUNCS_SINGLE),$(eval $(call FUNC_RULES_SINGLE,$(f))))
$(foreach f,$(FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT),$(eval $(call FUNC_RULES_DOUBLE_OPT,$(f))))
$(foreach f,$(FUNCS_DOUBLE_NOOPT),$(eval $(call FUNC_RULES_DOUBLE,$(f))))

# --- Top-level targets ---

OPT_ELFS = $(foreach f,$(FUNCS_SINGLE),$(TESTDIR)/test_$(f)_opt.elf) \
           $(foreach f,$(FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT),$(TESTDIR)/test_$(f)_opt.elf)
REF_ELFS = $(foreach f,$(FUNCS),$(TESTDIR)/test_$(f)_ref.elf)

all: $(OPT_ELFS) $(REF_ELFS)

test-all: $(foreach f,$(FUNCS),test-$(f))
	@echo "Done."

size-all: $(ASM_OBJS) $(S_ASM_OBJS)
	@echo "=== .text size (bytes) ==="
	@$(foreach o,$(ASM_OBJS) $(S_ASM_OBJS), \
		printf "  %-20s %d\n" $(basename $(o)) \
			$$($(SIZE) -A $(o) | awk '/\.text/ {print $$2}');)
	@echo "  --------------------"
	@printf "  %-20s %d\n" "TOTAL" \
		$$($(SIZE) -A $(ASM_OBJS) $(S_ASM_OBJS) | awk '/\.text/ {s+=$$2} END {print s}')

list:
	@echo "Targets: all size-all test-all clean list"
	@echo "Per-function: $(foreach f,$(FUNCS),test-$(f))"

clean:
	rm -f $(ASM_OBJS) $(S_ASM_OBJS) $(ANDES_OBJS) libandes.a $(TESTDIR)/*.o $(TESTDIR)/*.elf $(TESTDIR)/*_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/*_opt.txt

.PHONY: all size-all test-all clean list $(foreach f,$(FUNCS),test-$(f)) \
        $(foreach f,$(FUNCS_SINGLE),size-$(f)) \
        $(foreach f,$(FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT),size-$(f))

content.text:
All files are legitimate math library code. Analysis complete, delivering findings.

# File sizes and line counts

| File | Bytes | Lines |
|---|---|---|
| andes_dpreduct.s | 8754 | 218 |
| andes_fpreduct.s | 4095 | 102 |
| andes_dpexexp.s | 7227 | 205 |
| andes_dpexlog.s | 11029 | 309 |
| andes_dpexsqrt.s | 7655 | 184 |
| andes_dpexatan.s | 9794 | 312 |
| andes_fpexexp.s | 3752 | 106 |
| andes_fpexlog.s | 9802 | 283 |
| andes_fpexsqrt.s | 2660 | 71 |
| andes_fpexatan.s | 9966 | 301 |
| andes_r.s | 1316 | 53 |
| andes_r4.s | 8121 | 275 |
| andes_n.s | 4207 | 105 |
| andes_0.s | 4479 | 244 |
| andes_1.s | 4480 | 244 |

Built artifacts present in test/ (e.g. test_acos_opt.elf through cos/sinh/etc, timestamps ~Apr 7 14:36).

# Per-file analysis

## 1. andes_dpreduct.s (218 lines) - bulk work
Pure argument reduction loop, no helper calls. Function body dominates.
- Repeated 3-way idiom: `mv t6, xN; op xN,...; sltu t6, ...` appears 16+ times (carry-capture into t6). Not simplifiable without rewriting - this is how riscv lacks ADC.
- Many `tail .LLx` to labels 1-3 lines later that converge on a single `sltu`; branches like `.LL1/.LL2` structure could fall through instead of `tail`.
- `li x21, 2147483648` occurs at lines 19, and also at line 140 as different constant load. Line 85,100,121,183 all `li t6, 1` - identical 5 occurrences; could be hoisted once (though t6 is also clobbered as flag register between them, so only partial).
- No `li 0 + sw` opportunities (no stores in body).
- Line 212-215 `.LAnan` block: `li x14,-1; li x13,...; li x12,0; tail .LAret2` - `li x12,0` could be `mv x12, zero` (same size actually) but effectively can't shrink.
- Epilogue register restore sequence (lines 204-210): saves x9,x18-x21 (5 regs) but only x9,x18,x19,x20,x21 used. All necessary.
- Callee-saves actually clobbered: yes all used.

## 2. andes_fpreduct.s (102 lines) - bulk work
Same pattern as dpreduct but smaller/single-precision.
- Same 3-way mv/op/sltu idiom ~8 times.
- `li t6, 1` not seen; fewer hoistable constants.
- Line 98-100 `.LAnan`: `li x12,-1; li x11, 4290772992; tail .LAret` - no zero stores.
- Prologue saves x9,x18,x19 (3) - all used.

## 3. andes_dpexexp.s (205 lines) - helper calls + bulk
Calls `__gf_exmul`, `__gf_sigmax` twice. Two nearly identical save/restore+sigmax blocks (lines 129-152 and 154-176) - 24 lines each, differ only in table address (expcoo vs expcon). Could be factored to a shared helper + conditional `la` before `tail`/`call`.
- Duplicate 7-slot sw/lw save/restore around each call (lines 129-135 save, 145-151 restore; 154-160 save, 170-176 restore). Identical boilerplate x4.
- `li x21, 2147483648` at lines 124, 201 - duplicates.
- `.LAovr` (194-203): `li x18,0; ... li x20,0; mv x19,x20; ... li x19,2147483648; li x20,32767`. `li x18,0` could be omitted if x18 is then overwritten via `mv` (it isn't in one branch but is set to 0 in the other via `mv x19, x20` where x20 already 0). Minor.
- Stores-of-zero opportunities: none, stores are of live regs.
- The symmetric save/restore around the two `__gf_sigmax` calls is the clearest factoring win (~20+ instructions saved).

## 4. andes_dpexlog.s (309 lines) - helpers + bulk
- Calls __clzsi2 twice with identical 14-line save/restore scaffold (lines 119-133 and 207-221). Duplicate boilerplate.
- Calls __gf_exdiv, __gf_exmul x2, __gf_sigmax, __gf_exadd x2. Heavy save/restore around each.
- Large block 143-204 then 271-294 both save 7 words then restore 7 words around exadd/exmul chains - near-identical shapes.
- `li t6, 1` at lines 250 only once here.
- The bastab indexing at 185-190 does `add x21, x21, t6` three times (multiply by 12). Single `slli`+`add` could do: shift t6 by 1, add twice? Actually x*12 = (x<<3)+(x<<2); current 3-add form is already 3 ops (same as sh3add+add with Zba; without Zba, equivalent).
- Lots of `mv x20,x12; mv x19,x11; mv x18,x10` triplets (function-return stash) appearing 3+ times - pattern that could become a subroutine but callee-save overhead would exceed savings.
- Some `mv x21, x15; ...; mv x15, x21` (lines 228-233) - 3-way swap through x21 stash to free x15 as mul operand; x21 use is legitimate (preserving x5).

## 5. andes_dpexsqrt.s (184 lines) - bulk work
Digit-by-digit square root; no function calls.
- `li x9, 65535` appears at lines 55, 97, 116, 135 (four times). Clear hoist opportunity: keep in a callee-save across the whole function.
- `li x15, ...constant...` for two paths (1743919104/2466250752 at 16/30; 1293109248/1828715520 at 21/35) - these are alternative constants for even/odd exponent, cannot be hoisted (mutually exclusive paths) but the `mv x21, x14 / ... / mv x14, x21` save-restore idiom (17-20, 31-34) is identical between the two arms - factoring limited.
- Lines 18-20 and 32-34: `mv x21, x14; mul x14, ...; mulhu x15, ...; mv x14, x21` - this is the "stash x14, compute mul into x14/x15, restore x14" dead-result pattern. Crucially, `mul x14, ...` result is IMMEDIATELY overwritten by the next `mv x14, x21`. So `mul x14, x11, x15` is dead. This is the same pattern you optimized in s_sqrt.s. Present in BOTH arms (twice).
- Lines 130-131, 162-170 contain a second apparently-dead `mul`/`mulhu` pattern worth verifying.
- No `sw 0`/`li+sw` opportunities (no memory writes; function is leaf).
- Loop bodies .Lb3/.Lb5/.Lb7/.Lb9/.Lb11/.Lb13 are all identical 4-instruction shapes: `addi -1; add; sltu; beqz` - six copies. Factoring to macro would not reduce machine code (already minimum), but the surrounding setup (div/remu/mul/slli/mv/sub/sltu) is also repeated 4 times (lines 45-64, 71-86, 94-111, 113-128, 133-147, 149-161). This is structural code duplication of ~18 instructions x 4-6 copies; strong refactor candidate but high risk.

## 6. andes_dpexatan.s (312 lines) - helpers + bulk
- `li x21, 2147483648` / `li x20, 2147483648` / `li x18, 2147483648` (lines 147, 208, 287) - same constant loaded 3 times into different regs.
- `li x13,...; li x14,...; li x15, 1023` setup repeated (lines 139-141, 291-293 with different magic numbers for __gf_exsub/exdiv args).
- Large save/restore blocks around call __gf_exdiv, __gf_exmul x2, __gf_sigmax, __gf_exadd, __gf_exsub. Epilogue (lines 295-300) reloads 6 regs just for an `and`+`beqz` then another call - the reload is partially wasted since x18 is immediately overwritten line 287.
- Line 139 `li x10, 0` could be `mv x10, zero` (same size). 
- Lines 174-178 `mv x21,x18; mul x9,x18,x13; mulhu x18,x18,x13; mv x9,x18; mv x18,x21` - classic x9 stash, then `mul x9,...` result overwritten by `mv x9, x18` - **dead `mul` instruction** (same pattern as dpexsqrt and the one you already fixed in s_sqrt).
- Lines 179-184: same `mv x21, x15; mulhu x15, ...; mul x14, ...; mv x13,x14; mv x14,x15; mv x15,x21` - here both mul results (x14 and x15) are used after the re-swap (x13 gets x14, x14 gets x15). x15 is restored from x21 to save it. This pattern is 3-way shuffle to work around operand constraints - probably necessary.

## 7. andes_fpexexp.s (106 lines) - mostly bulk
- `mv x10, x11; mv x11, x20` (lines 77-78) - pattern after mul/mulhu. Line 76 `mul x10, x18, x10` result is immediately overwritten by `mv x10, x11` - **dead mul** (classic pattern).
- Loop `.Lb5` is a 7-call polynomial evaluator reading from `expcof`.
- `li t6, -1` at line 62, and `li x20, 7/32/65535` - no dup.
- Small enough that helper-inlining won't help.

## 8. andes_fpexlog.s (283 lines) - helpers + bulk
- Calls __clzsi2 twice (lines 92, 225) with identical 14-line pushm/popm scaffolds.
- `li x21, 2147483648` appears at lines 71, 82, 211, 261 - **four duplicate loads of the same constant**. Could hoist into callee-save for the whole function.
- `li x21, 65535` at 111, `li x21, 32` at 151, 195, 251 (three times), `li x21, 1073741824` at 170 - some repeats.
- Line 113 `mul x12, x20, x19` result overwritten on next iteration of loop? Looking more carefully: line 127 `divu x16, x10, x14; ... add x10, x16, 0` - this is a strange `add x10, x16, 0` (alias for mv) after a divu where the original pseudoinst would be `divr x16, x14, x10, x14`. Not dead, just verbose mv.
- Line 162-166: `mulhu x16, x13, x12; mul x12,x13,x12; addi x13, x16, 0; mv x12, x13; mv x13, x21` - `mul x12, ...` result is killed by `mv x12, x13` two lines later - **dead mul** (another instance).
- Line 238-240: `mul x12, x13, x15; mulhu x13, x13, x15; mv x19, x12; mv x12, x21` - `mul x12` result saved to x19 so NOT dead. Good.
- Line 175: `mul x10, x10, x12` result overwritten by `mv x10, x11` - **dead mul**.
- Epilogue at 273-280 does a double-pop (32 bytes + 16 bytes) because prologue pushed in two pieces. Could combine.
- Boilerplate save/restore around both __clzsi2 calls identical.

## 9. andes_fpexsqrt.s (71 lines) - bulk work, compact
- Lines 48-49 `mulhu x15, x13, x9; mul x14, x13, x9` then x14 not used until line 52 where it's overwritten - **dead mul x14** at line 49.
- Line 51-52: `mulhu x15, x9, x18; mul x14, x9, x18` - x14 then used line 53? No, line 53 uses x15. x14 appears dead again. **Possibly dead mul**.
- Line 53-54: `mulhu x11, x15, x10; mul x10, x15, x10` - x10 then used line 57 via `x13+x11` (not x10). Actually line 57 `add x10, x13, x11` overwrites x10. So `mul x10,...` at 54 is **dead**.
- Very compact function, multiple dead muls. Strong candidate.
- No li+sw or constant duplication.

## 10. andes_fpexatan.s (301 lines) - bulk work with table lookup
- `li x15, 2147483648` at lines 86, 155, 270 - 3 duplicates.
- `li x17, 8` at 134, 157; `li x17, 126` at 88; `li x17, 127` at 148, 278; `li x17, 129` at 135; `li x17, 252` at 215; `li x17, 32` at 203; `li x17, 1023` at 148 (wait that's wrong - let me recheck) - line 148 is `subri $r5, $r1, #127`. Actually 88, 148, 278 all have 127. And 157 has 8. Yes, several duplicate small constant loads in x17.
- Same divu/remu/mul/slli/sub/sltu/.Lb loop pattern as dpexsqrt, appears 4 times (lines 97-133, 172-207). Structural duplication.
- Lines 118 `mul x14, x14, x13` - used at 121. OK.
- Line 145-147: `mulhu x15, x19, x10; mul x14, x19, x10; mv x19, x15` - `mul x14, ...` not subsequently used before overwrite at line 157? Line 149 uses x15 not x14, line 157 `li x17, 8`. Actually x14 is next used at line 156 `srl x13, x13, x14` - wait that's x14 as shift-amount. Or line 158 `sub x14, x17, x9` - overwrite. So between 146 and 158, is x14 read? Line 147 `mv x19, x15`, 148 li, 149 sub x15, 150 sltiu, 151 bne, 152 addi x19, 154 srl x19, 155 li, 156 or x19, 157 li x17, 158 sub x14 - **x14 is dead between 146 and 158. Dead mul.**
- Line 212-213: `mulhu x15, x10, x10; mul x14, x10, x10` - x14 next used? 214 `mv x19, x15`, 215 li, 216 sub, 217 sub, 218 sltiu, 219 bne, 220 addi x19, 222 srl x19, 223 li x15, 224 `mul x14, x19, x15` - x14 overwritten. **Dead mul at 213.**
- Line 228-229: `mul x14,...; mulhu x15,...` then 230 `li x14, ...` - **dead mul at 228.**
- Line 240-243: `mulhu x11, x10, x15; mul x10, x10, x15; mv x10, x11; mv x11, x9` - `mul x10,...` overwritten by mv - **dead mul**.
- Strong density of dead mul/mulhu pairs across the fpex* files.

## 11. andes_r.s (53 lines) - pure helper dispatcher
Not bulk work. Just: load table ptr, call jalr, call p4ac, save result, call p6, restore, call __divdf3, return. Minimal. No optimization surface except: x9/x18 are saved via caller's frame (sw at 4(sp)/8(sp)) but the file has no prologue/epilogue for x9/x18 visible - it's relying on a caller's saved area (tail-called from another function with pre-allocated frame). Fragile but intentional.
- No dup constants, no dead code, nothing to shrink.

## 12. andes_r4.s (275 lines) - 5 functions (andes_r0..r4) + 4 data tables
Each function is a helper dispatcher chain:
- andes_r0: 17 insns, very short
- andes_r1: ~50 insns, has shared `.L24` tail that andes_r2 branches to - **already factored**
- andes_r2: 8 insns, tail-calls .L24
- andes_r3: ~20 insns
- andes_r4: 22 insns
- `lw x12, 32(sp); lw x13, 36(sp)` - loads of saved a/b arg pair repeated 4 times (lines 170/177/182/209/266/269). These are legitimate reloads across call boundaries.

- `li x10, 0; lui x11, 261888; call __divdf3` appears twice (lines 150-152 in andes_r0, and nearby). This is `1.0 / x` pattern; not trivially hoistable since it's distinct calls.

- andes_r1 lines 199-201: `mv x10, x12; lui x15, 524288; xor x11, x13, x15; jalr x21` - sign flip then indirect call. Fine.
- Prologue for each function saves only ra at 28(sp) - frame is caller-allocated (like andes_r.s).
- No `li+sw` zero; no dead code visible.
- `jalr x21` (indirect call to a function pointer in x21) used in r0, r1 (twice), r4 (twice). This is the p-chain convention.

## 13. andes_n.s (105 lines) - 2 functions qn, pn (helpers for jn/yn bessel)
- Two functions, qn and pn, very similar structure. ~45 insns each. 
- Both begin with identical 13-line classification/branch chain (lines 9-22 and 62-75): compare |x1| against 262656, 1074933387, 1074191213; each `slt`+`beqz`+`addi fp,fp,-104` (or -96 in pn). **Near-identical 13-line boilerplate duplicated** differing only in constant `-104` vs `-96`.
- Lines 53-54 and 101-102: `addi sp, sp, 16; addi sp, sp, 16` - two adjacent sp-adjustments (one decrement and one increment, or two increments) left over from mechanical translation - could merge into single `addi sp, sp, 32`. **Duplicate/foldable sp adjustment.**
- Line 54 comment says `addi $sp, $sp, #16` but line 102 comment says `addi $sp, $sp, #8` - inconsistent; line 102 appears to be a bug where the intended second addi is 8 but coded as 16. Functionally this may be wrong, worth verifying.
- Epilogue load sequence lw x9,x18,x8,ra (lines 49-52, 97-100) is standard.
- The two classification blocks are a clear factoring opportunity (save ~12 instructions if turned into a helper).

## 14. andes_0.s (244 lines) - qzero + pzero, mostly data
- 2 functions, tiny: qzero (12 insns), pzero (11 insns). Just prologue + `la fp, Q+312` + `tail qn`/`pn`.
- Data tables Q (416 bytes) and P (384 bytes) dominate file.
- Both functions have `addi sp, sp, -16; addi sp, sp, -16` (lines 219-220, 235-236) - **duplicate/foldable sp adjustments**, should be single `addi sp, sp, -32`. 4 foldings total across file.
- No other opportunities.

## 15. andes_1.s (244 lines) - qone + pone, mostly data
- Structurally identical to andes_0.s with different constants. Same `addi sp,sp,-16; addi sp,sp,-16` doubled decrement in both qone and pone (lines 219-220, 235-236). **Same foldable sp adjustment** - 4 foldings.
- No other ops.

# Cross-cutting patterns summary

**Dead mul/mulhu pairs (same pattern you fixed in s_sqrt):**
- andes_dpexsqrt.s: lines 18-20, 32-34 (both arms), potentially 99-100
- andes_dpexatan.s: lines 175-178 (clearest)
- andes_fpexexp.s: lines 75-78
- andes_fpexlog.s: lines 162-166, 174-175
- andes_fpexsqrt.s: lines 48-49, 51-52, 53-54 (three in 7 lines!)
- andes_fpexatan.s: lines 145-146, 212-213, 228-229, 240-241

**Duplicate `li` constants (hoistable into callee-save):**
- andes_dpexlog.s: `li x9, 65535` x4 in dpexsqrt; `li x21, 2147483648` x2 in dpexexp
- andes_fpexlog.s: `li x21, 2147483648` x4
- andes_fpexatan.s: `li x15, 2147483648` x3, `li x17, 127` x3

**Duplicate save/restore boilerplate around helper calls:**
- andes_dpexexp.s: two near-identical 24-line blocks around __gf_sigmax
- andes_dpexlog.s: two 14-line __clzsi2 scaffolds
- andes_fpexlog.s: two 14-line __clzsi2 scaffolds

**Foldable adjacent `addi sp, sp, -16` pairs:**
- andes_0.s: 4 occurrences
- andes_1.s: 4 occurrences
- andes_n.s: 2 epilogue + suspicious comment mismatch

**Duplicate classification prologue:**
- andes_n.s: qn and pn share a 13-line classification block

**Structural duplication of divu/remu/mul/sltu/loop pattern:**
- andes_dpexsqrt.s: 4-6 copies (~18 insns each)
- andes_fpexatan.s: 4 copies
- andes_fpexlog.s: 2 copies
(High-value but high-risk refactor.)

**No li+sw zero opportunities found in any of these files** (the assembly is mostly leaf/callee code without the li-0-then-store patterns that were present in s_expm1/s_log1p/s_fmod).

**No arg-swap before __adddf3/__subdf3/__muldf3 patterns** - none of these files call soft-float routines except the dispatcher files (andes_r/r4/n/0/1), which already order args correctly.

**No unused callee-saved register saves spotted** - every saved x9/x18/x19/x20/x21 is used.

**Inlining-candidate tail calls:** `tail .Le1` `tail .LL3` etc. exist in dpreduct, fpreduct, dpexsqrt, fpexexp - these are control-flow tails to labels 1-3 lines away that could often become fall-through if blocks were reordered. Marginal.

# Priority ranking for size reduction

1. **fpexsqrt.s** - 3 dead muls in 7 lines, very small file, easy win
2. **fpexatan.s** - 4 dead muls + 3x constant dup
3. **dpexsqrt.s** - 4+ dead muls + 4x `li x9, 65535` + structural duplication
4. **fpexlog.s** - 2-3 dead muls + 4x `li 2147483648` + duplicate __clzsi2 scaffolds
5. **dpexexp.s** - duplicate sigmax call scaffolds (biggest single factoring)
6. **andes_0.s / andes_1.s** - 4x each foldable sp-adjust pairs (tiny but zero-risk)
7. **andes_n.s** - suspicious sp-adjust + duplicate classification prologue (verify line 102 first)
8. **dpexatan.s** - 1 clear dead mul + constant dups
9. **fpexexp.s** - 1 dead mul
10. **dpexlog.s** - duplicate __clzsi2 scaffolds, bastab 3-add indexing
11. **dpreduct.s / fpreduct.s** - mostly already tight; marginal improvements only
12. **andes_r.s / andes_r4.s** - dispatcher code, nothing to cut

content.text:
All files are legitimate math library code. Analysis complete, delivering findings.

# File sizes and line counts

| File | Bytes | Lines |
|---|---|---|
| andes_dpreduct.s | 8754 | 218 |
| andes_fpreduct.s | 4095 | 102 |
| andes_dpexexp.s | 7227 | 205 |
| andes_dpexlog.s | 11029 | 309 |
| andes_dpexsqrt.s | 7655 | 184 |
| andes_dpexatan.s | 9794 | 312 |
| andes_fpexexp.s | 3752 | 106 |
| andes_fpexlog.s | 9802 | 283 |
| andes_fpexsqrt.s | 2660 | 71 |
| andes_fpexatan.s | 9966 | 301 |
| andes_r.s | 1316 | 53 |
| andes_r4.s | 8121 | 275 |
| andes_n.s | 4207 | 105 |
| andes_0.s | 4479 | 244 |
| andes_1.s | 4480 | 244 |

Built artifacts present in test/ (e.g. test_acos_opt.elf through cos/sinh/etc, timestamps ~Apr 7 14:36).

# Per-file analysis

## 1. andes_dpreduct.s (218 lines) - bulk work
Pure argument reduction loop, no helper calls. Function body dominates.
- Repeated 3-way idiom: `mv t6, xN; op xN,...; sltu t6, ...` appears 16+ times (carry-capture into t6). Not simplifiable without rewriting - this is how riscv lacks ADC.
- Many `tail .LLx` to labels 1-3 lines later that converge on a single `sltu`; branches like `.LL1/.LL2` structure could fall through instead of `tail`.
- `li x21, 2147483648` occurs at lines 19, and also at line 140 as different constant load. Line 85,100,121,183 all `li t6, 1` - identical 5 occurrences; could be hoisted once (though t6 is also clobbered as flag register between them, so only partial).
- No `li 0 + sw` opportunities (no stores in body).
- Line 212-215 `.LAnan` block: `li x14,-1; li x13,...; li x12,0; tail .LAret2` - `li x12,0` could be `mv x12, zero` (same size actually) but effectively can't shrink.
- Epilogue register restore sequence (lines 204-210): saves x9,x18-x21 (5 regs) but only x9,x18,x19,x20,x21 used. All necessary.
- Callee-saves actually clobbered: yes all used.

## 2. andes_fpreduct.s (102 lines) - bulk work
Same pattern as dpreduct but smaller/single-precision.
- Same 3-way mv/op/sltu idiom ~8 times.
- `li t6, 1` not seen; fewer hoistable constants.
- Line 98-100 `.LAnan`: `li x12,-1; li x11, 4290772992; tail .LAret` - no zero stores.
- Prologue saves x9,x18,x19 (3) - all used.

## 3. andes_dpexexp.s (205 lines) - helper calls + bulk
Calls `__gf_exmul`, `__gf_sigmax` twice. Two nearly identical save/restore+sigmax blocks (lines 129-152 and 154-176) - 24 lines each, differ only in table address (expcoo vs expcon). Could be factored to a shared helper + conditional `la` before `tail`/`call`.
- Duplicate 7-slot sw/lw save/restore around each call (lines 129-135 save, 145-151 restore; 154-160 save, 170-176 restore). Identical boilerplate x4.
- `li x21, 2147483648` at lines 124, 201 - duplicates.
- `.LAovr` (194-203): `li x18,0; ... li x20,0; mv x19,x20; ... li x19,2147483648; li x20,32767`. `li x18,0` could be omitted if x18 is then overwritten via `mv` (it isn't in one branch but is set to 0 in the other via `mv x19, x20` where x20 already 0). Minor.
- Stores-of-zero opportunities: none, stores are of live regs.
- The symmetric save/restore around the two `__gf_sigmax` calls is the clearest factoring win (~20+ instructions saved).

## 4. andes_dpexlog.s (309 lines) - helpers + bulk
- Calls __clzsi2 twice with identical 14-line save/restore scaffold (lines 119-133 and 207-221). Duplicate boilerplate.
- Calls __gf_exdiv, __gf_exmul x2, __gf_sigmax, __gf_exadd x2. Heavy save/restore around each.
- Large block 143-204 then 271-294 both save 7 words then restore 7 words around exadd/exmul chains - near-identical shapes.
- `li t6, 1` at lines 250 only once here.
- The bastab indexing at 185-190 does `add x21, x21, t6` three times (multiply by 12). Single `slli`+`add` could do: shift t6 by 1, add twice? Actually x*12 = (x<<3)+(x<<2); current 3-add form is already 3 ops (same as sh3add+add with Zba; without Zba, equivalent).
- Lots of `mv x20,x12; mv x19,x11; mv x18,x10` triplets (function-return stash) appearing 3+ times - pattern that could become a subroutine but callee-save overhead would exceed savings.
- Some `mv x21, x15; ...; mv x15, x21` (lines 228-233) - 3-way swap through x21 stash to free x15 as mul operand; x21 use is legitimate (preserving x5).

## 5. andes_dpexsqrt.s (184 lines) - bulk work
Digit-by-digit square root; no function calls.
- `li x9, 65535` appears at lines 55, 97, 116, 135 (four times). Clear hoist opportunity: keep in a callee-save across the whole function.
- `li x15, ...constant...` for two paths (1743919104/2466250752 at 16/30; 1293109248/1828715520 at 21/35) - these are alternative constants for even/odd exponent, cannot be hoisted (mutually exclusive paths) but the `mv x21, x14 / ... / mv x14, x21` save-restore idiom (17-20, 31-34) is identical between the two arms - factoring limited.
- Lines 18-20 and 32-34: `mv x21, x14; mul x14, ...; mulhu x15, ...; mv x14, x21` - this is the "stash x14, compute mul into x14/x15, restore x14" dead-result pattern. Crucially, `mul x14, ...` result is IMMEDIATELY overwritten by the next `mv x14, x21`. So `mul x14, x11, x15` is dead. This is the same pattern you optimized in s_sqrt.s. Present in BOTH arms (twice).
- Lines 130-131, 162-170 contain a second apparently-dead `mul`/`mulhu` pattern worth verifying.
- No `sw 0`/`li+sw` opportunities (no memory writes; function is leaf).
- Loop bodies .Lb3/.Lb5/.Lb7/.Lb9/.Lb11/.Lb13 are all identical 4-instruction shapes: `addi -1; add; sltu; beqz` - six copies. Factoring to macro would not reduce machine code (already minimum), but the surrounding setup (div/remu/mul/slli/mv/sub/sltu) is also repeated 4 times (lines 45-64, 71-86, 94-111, 113-128, 133-147, 149-161). This is structural code duplication of ~18 instructions x 4-6 copies; strong refactor candidate but high risk.

## 6. andes_dpexatan.s (312 lines) - helpers + bulk
- `li x21, 2147483648` / `li x20, 2147483648` / `li x18, 2147483648` (lines 147, 208, 287) - same constant loaded 3 times into different regs.
- `li x13,...; li x14,...; li x15, 1023` setup repeated (lines 139-141, 291-293 with different magic numbers for __gf_exsub/exdiv args).
- Large save/restore blocks around call __gf_exdiv, __gf_exmul x2, __gf_sigmax, __gf_exadd, __gf_exsub. Epilogue (lines 295-300) reloads 6 regs just for an `and`+`beqz` then another call - the reload is partially wasted since x18 is immediately overwritten line 287.
- Line 139 `li x10, 0` could be `mv x10, zero` (same size). 
- Lines 174-178 `mv x21,x18; mul x9,x18,x13; mulhu x18,x18,x13; mv x9,x18; mv x18,x21` - classic x9 stash, then `mul x9,...` result overwritten by `mv x9, x18` - **dead `mul` instruction** (same pattern as dpexsqrt and the one you already fixed in s_sqrt).
- Lines 179-184: same `mv x21, x15; mulhu x15, ...; mul x14, ...; mv x13,x14; mv x14,x15; mv x15,x21` - here both mul results (x14 and x15) are used after the re-swap (x13 gets x14, x14 gets x15). x15 is restored from x21 to save it. This pattern is 3-way shuffle to work around operand constraints - probably necessary.

## 7. andes_fpexexp.s (106 lines) - mostly bulk
- `mv x10, x11; mv x11, x20` (lines 77-78) - pattern after mul/mulhu. Line 76 `mul x10, x18, x10` result is immediately overwritten by `mv x10, x11` - **dead mul** (classic pattern).
- Loop `.Lb5` is a 7-call polynomial evaluator reading from `expcof`.
- `li t6, -1` at line 62, and `li x20, 7/32/65535` - no dup.
- Small enough that helper-inlining won't help.

## 8. andes_fpexlog.s (283 lines) - helpers + bulk
- Calls __clzsi2 twice (lines 92, 225) with identical 14-line pushm/popm scaffolds.
- `li x21, 2147483648` appears at lines 71, 82, 211, 261 - **four duplicate loads of the same constant**. Could hoist into callee-save for the whole function.
- `li x21, 65535` at 111, `li x21, 32` at 151, 195, 251 (three times), `li x21, 1073741824` at 170 - some repeats.
- Line 113 `mul x12, x20, x19` result overwritten on next iteration of loop? Looking more carefully: line 127 `divu x16, x10, x14; ... add x10, x16, 0` - this is a strange `add x10, x16, 0` (alias for mv) after a divu where the original pseudoinst would be `divr x16, x14, x10, x14`. Not dead, just verbose mv.
- Line 162-166: `mulhu x16, x13, x12; mul x12,x13,x12; addi x13, x16, 0; mv x12, x13; mv x13, x21` - `mul x12, ...` result is killed by `mv x12, x13` two lines later - **dead mul** (another instance).
- Line 238-240: `mul x12, x13, x15; mulhu x13, x13, x15; mv x19, x12; mv x12, x21` - `mul x12` result saved to x19 so NOT dead. Good.
- Line 175: `mul x10, x10, x12` result overwritten by `mv x10, x11` - **dead mul**.
- Epilogue at 273-280 does a double-pop (32 bytes + 16 bytes) because prologue pushed in two pieces. Could combine.
- Boilerplate save/restore around both __clzsi2 calls identical.

## 9. andes_fpexsqrt.s (71 lines) - bulk work, compact
- Lines 48-49 `mulhu x15, x13, x9; mul x14, x13, x9` then x14 not used until line 52 where it's overwritten - **dead mul x14** at line 49.
- Line 51-52: `mulhu x15, x9, x18; mul x14, x9, x18` - x14 then used line 53? No, line 53 uses x15. x14 appears dead again. **Possibly dead mul**.
- Line 53-54: `mulhu x11, x15, x10; mul x10, x15, x10` - x10 then used line 57 via `x13+x11` (not x10). Actually line 57 `add x10, x13, x11` overwrites x10. So `mul x10,...` at 54 is **dead**.
- Very compact function, multiple dead muls. Strong candidate.
- No li+sw or constant duplication.

## 10. andes_fpexatan.s (301 lines) - bulk work with table lookup
- `li x15, 2147483648` at lines 86, 155, 270 - 3 duplicates.
- `li x17, 8` at 134, 157; `li x17, 126` at 88; `li x17, 127` at 148, 278; `li x17, 129` at 135; `li x17, 252` at 215; `li x17, 32` at 203; `li x17, 1023` at 148 (wait that's wrong - let me recheck) - line 148 is `subri $r5, $r1, #127`. Actually 88, 148, 278 all have 127. And 157 has 8. Yes, several duplicate small constant loads in x17.
- Same divu/remu/mul/slli/sub/sltu/.Lb loop pattern as dpexsqrt, appears 4 times (lines 97-133, 172-207). Structural duplication.
- Lines 118 `mul x14, x14, x13` - used at 121. OK.
- Line 145-147: `mulhu x15, x19, x10; mul x14, x19, x10; mv x19, x15` - `mul x14, ...` not subsequently used before overwrite at line 157? Line 149 uses x15 not x14, line 157 `li x17, 8`. Actually x14 is next used at line 156 `srl x13, x13, x14` - wait that's x14 as shift-amount. Or line 158 `sub x14, x17, x9` - overwrite. So between 146 and 158, is x14 read? Line 147 `mv x19, x15`, 148 li, 149 sub x15, 150 sltiu, 151 bne, 152 addi x19, 154 srl x19, 155 li, 156 or x19, 157 li x17, 158 sub x14 - **x14 is dead between 146 and 158. Dead mul.**
- Line 212-213: `mulhu x15, x10, x10; mul x14, x10, x10` - x14 next used? 214 `mv x19, x15`, 215 li, 216 sub, 217 sub, 218 sltiu, 219 bne, 220 addi x19, 222 srl x19, 223 li x15, 224 `mul x14, x19, x15` - x14 overwritten. **Dead mul at 213.**
- Line 228-229: `mul x14,...; mulhu x15,...` then 230 `li x14, ...` - **dead mul at 228.**
- Line 240-243: `mulhu x11, x10, x15; mul x10, x10, x15; mv x10, x11; mv x11, x9` - `mul x10,...` overwritten by mv - **dead mul**.
- Strong density of dead mul/mulhu pairs across the fpex* files.

## 11. andes_r.s (53 lines) - pure helper dispatcher
Not bulk work. Just: load table ptr, call jalr, call p4ac, save result, call p6, restore, call __divdf3, return. Minimal. No optimization surface except: x9/x18 are saved via caller's frame (sw at 4(sp)/8(sp)) but the file has no prologue/epilogue for x9/x18 visible - it's relying on a caller's saved area (tail-called from another function with pre-allocated frame). Fragile but intentional.
- No dup constants, no dead code, nothing to shrink.

## 12. andes_r4.s (275 lines) - 5 functions (andes_r0..r4) + 4 data tables
Each function is a helper dispatcher chain:
- andes_r0: 17 insns, very short
- andes_r1: ~50 insns, has shared `.L24` tail that andes_r2 branches to - **already factored**
- andes_r2: 8 insns, tail-calls .L24
- andes_r3: ~20 insns
- andes_r4: 22 insns
- `lw x12, 32(sp); lw x13, 36(sp)` - loads of saved a/b arg pair repeated 4 times (lines 170/177/182/209/266/269). These are legitimate reloads across call boundaries.
- `lw x12, 40(sp); lw x13, 44(sp)` - same pattern another 3 times.
- `li x10, 0; lui x11, 261888; call __divdf3` appears twice (lines 150-152 in andes_r0, and nearby). This is `1.0 / x` pattern; not trivially hoistable since it's distinct calls.
- `lui x15, 524288; xor x11, x13, x15` (line 199-200) is a sign-flip; appears once.
- andes_r1 lines 199-201: `mv x10, x12; lui x15, 524288; xor x11, x13, x15; jalr x21` - sign flip then indirect call. Fine.
- Prologue for each function saves only ra at 28(sp) - frame is caller-allocated (like andes_r.s).
- No `li+sw` zero; no dead code visible.
- `jalr x21` (indirect call to a function pointer in x21) used in r0, r1 (twice), r4 (twice). This is the p-chain convention.

## 13. andes_n.s (105 lines) - 2 functions qn, pn (helpers for jn/yn bessel)
- Two functions, qn and pn, very similar structure. ~45 insns each. 
- Both begin with identical 13-line classification/branch chain (lines 9-22 and 62-75): compare |x1| against 262656, 1074933387, 1074191213; each `slt`+`beqz`+`addi fp,fp,-104` (or -96 in pn). **Near-identical 13-line boilerplate duplicated** differing only in constant `-104` vs `-96`.
- Lines 53-54 and 101-102: `addi sp, sp, 16; addi sp, sp, 16` - two adjacent sp-adjustments (one decrement and one increment, or two increments) left over from mechanical translation - could merge into single `addi sp, sp, 32`. **Duplicate/foldable sp adjustment.**
- Line 54 comment says `addi $sp, $sp, #16` but line 102 comment says `addi $sp, $sp, #8` - inconsistent; line 102 appears to be a bug where the intended second addi is 8 but coded as 16. Functionally this may be wrong, worth verifying.
- Epilogue load sequence lw x9,x18,x8,ra (lines 49-52, 97-100) is standard.
- The two classification blocks are a clear factoring opportunity (save ~12 instructions if turned into a helper).

## 14. andes_0.s (244 lines) - qzero + pzero, mostly data
- 2 functions, tiny: qzero (12 insns), pzero (11 insns). Just prologue + `la fp, Q+312` + `tail qn`/`pn`.
- Data tables Q (416 bytes) and P (384 bytes) dominate file.
- Both functions have `addi sp, sp, -16; addi sp, sp, -16` (lines 219-220, 235-236) - **duplicate/foldable sp adjustments**, should be single `addi sp, sp, -32`. 4 foldings total across file.
- No other opportunities.

## 15. andes_1.s (244 lines) - qone + pone, mostly data
- Structurally identical to andes_0.s with different constants. Same `addi sp,sp,-16; addi sp,sp,-16` doubled decrement in both qone and pone (lines 219-220, 235-236). **Same foldable sp adjustment** - 4 foldings.
- No other ops.

# Cross-cutting patterns summary

**Dead mul/mulhu pairs (same pattern you fixed in s_sqrt):**
- andes_dpexsqrt.s: lines 18-20, 32-34 (both arms), potentially 99-100
- andes_dpexatan.s: lines 175-178 (clearest)
- andes_fpexexp.s: lines 75-78
- andes_fpexlog.s: lines 162-166, 174-175
- andes_fpexsqrt.s: lines 48-49, 51-52, 53-54 (three in 7 lines!)
- andes_fpexatan.s: lines 145-146, 212-213, 228-229, 240-241

**Duplicate `li` constants (hoistable into callee-save):**
- andes_dpexlog.s: `li x9, 65535` x4 in dpexsqrt; `li x21, 2147483648` x2 in dpexexp
- andes_fpexlog.s: `li x21, 2147483648` x4
- andes_fpexatan.s: `li x15, 2147483648` x3, `li x17, 127` x3

**Duplicate save/restore boilerplate around helper calls:**
- andes_dpexexp.s: two near-identical 24-line blocks around __gf_sigmax
- andes_dpexlog.s: two 14-line __clzsi2 scaffolds
- andes_fpexlog.s: two 14-line __clzsi2 scaffolds

**Foldable adjacent `addi sp, sp, -16` pairs:**
- andes_0.s: 4 occurrences
- andes_1.s: 4 occurrences
- andes_n.s: 2 epilogue + suspicious comment mismatch

**Duplicate classification prologue:**
- andes_n.s: qn and pn share a 13-line classification block

**Structural duplication of divu/remu/mul/sltu/loop pattern:**
- andes_dpexsqrt.s: 4-6 copies (~18 insns each)
- andes_fpexatan.s: 4 copies
- andes_fpexlog.s: 2 copies
(High-value but high-risk refactor.)

**No li+sw zero opportunities found in any of these files** (the assembly is mostly leaf/callee code without the li-0-then-store patterns that were present in s_expm1/s_log1p/s_fmod).

**No arg-swap before __adddf3/__subdf3/__muldf3 patterns** - none of these files call soft-float routines except the dispatcher files (andes_r/r4/n/0/1), which already order args correctly.

**No unused callee-saved register saves spotted** - every saved x9/x18/x19/x20/x21 is used.

**Inlining-candidate tail calls:** `tail .Le1` `tail .LL3` etc. exist in dpreduct, fpreduct, dpexsqrt, fpexexp - these are control-flow tails to labels 1-3 lines away that could often become fall-through if blocks were reordered. Marginal.

# Priority ranking for size reduction

1. **fpexsqrt.s** - 3 dead muls in 7 lines, very small file, easy win
2. **fpexatan.s** - 4 dead muls + 3x constant dup
3. **dpexsqrt.s** - 4+ dead muls + 4x `li x9, 65535` + structural duplication
4. **fpexlog.s** - 2-3 dead muls + 4x `li 2147483648` + duplicate __clzsi2 scaffolds
5. **dpexexp.s** - duplicate sigmax call scaffolds (biggest single factoring)
6. **andes_0.s / andes_1.s** - 4x each foldable sp-adjust pairs (tiny but zero-risk)
7. **andes_n.s** - suspicious sp-adjust + duplicate classification prologue (verify line 102 first)
8. **dpexatan.s** - 1 clear dead mul + constant dups
9. **fpexexp.s** - 1 dead mul
10. **dpexlog.s** - duplicate __clzsi2 scaffolds, bastab 3-add indexing
11. **dpreduct.s / fpreduct.s** - mostly already tight; marginal improvements only
12. **andes_r.s / andes_r4.s** - dispatcher code, nothing to cut

input.content:
# Plan: Andes helper size optimizations

## Context

The RISC-V libm under `libm/machine/riscv/` has ~40 hand-written `andes_*.s`
helper files shared by all higher-level math functions. Prior work has been
focused on the `s_*.s` / `sf_*.s` top-level routines (dead-mul removal,
`sw x0` for zero stores, constant hoisting). The helper files were not yet
audited. This plan is the result of a size audit of the top-level helpers
(not rv32e). The goal is to remove dead code and boilerplate duplication
without touching behavior, verified via the existing `make test-*` A/B
comparison.

## Findings - candidate edits

Ranked by risk (low to higher) and by estimated byte savings.

### Tier A - trivial, mechanical, zero behavioral risk

1. **andes_0.s** - 4x foldable adjacent `addi sp, sp, -16` pairs.
   Each pair (two separate qzero / pzero prologues/epilogues) collapses to a
   single `addi sp, sp, -32` / `+32`. Est. 8 instructions removed.
   File lines ~219-220, ~235-236 (and matching epilogues).

2. **andes_1.s** - identical pattern to andes_0.s. Est. 8 instructions removed.

3. **andes_n.s** - two adjacent `addi sp, sp, 16` at lines ~53-54 and
   ~101-102. Investigate the line 102 `16 vs 8` comment mismatch first -
   may be a latent bug in the hand-translated code or just a stale comment.
   If comment is stale, fold to single `addi sp, sp, 32`. Est. 2
   instructions removed.

4. **andes_fpexsqrt.s** - three apparently-dead `mul` instructions in a
   7-line span (lines 48-49, 51-52, 53-54): each writes to `x14` or `x10`
   that is overwritten before any read. Same dead-`mul` pattern fixed in
   `s_sqrt.s` commit c40cb6ed9. Est. 3 instructions removed in the
   smallest helper.

### Tier B - dead `mul` removals in other helpers

Apply the same pattern used in `s_sqrt.s`: delete `mul` instructions whose
destination is overwritten before use. Each must be individually verified
against surrounding register life by reading a 10-line window and then by
`make test-<func>` for every function that reaches the helper.

5. **andes_fpexatan.s** - ~4 dead muls at lines ~145-146, ~212-213,
   ~228-229, ~240-241. Also 3x duplicate `li x15, 2147483648` across
   lines ~86, 155, 270 (hoistable into a callee-save x-reg spanning the
   whole function body if a free callee-save exists - needs liveness
   verification, else skip).

6. **andes_dpexsqrt.s** - ~4 dead muls in the even/odd exponent arms
   (lines 18-20, 32-34). Plus 4x `li x9, 65535` at lines 55, 97, 116,
   135 - hoist to a single load at function entry if x9 is preserved.

7. **andes_fpexlog.s** - ~2 dead muls (lines ~162-166, ~174-175). Plus
   4x duplicate `li x21, 2147483648` - hoist. Also prologue/epilogue
   pushes sp twice (split pushm pattern); combining into a single frame
   adjust saves 2 instructions.

8. **andes_dpexatan.s** - 1 clearly dead `mul` at line ~175-178.
   Plus multiple duplicate `li ..., 2147483648` loads.

9. **andes_fpexexp.s** - 1 dead `mul` at line ~75-78 (same pattern).

### Tier C - duplicate scaffolding (bigger wins, higher risk)

10. **andes_dpexlog.s** - two identical 14-line push/call `__clzsi2` /
    pop scaffolds. Consider factoring to a local helper block or using
    fall-through to a shared tail. Est. ~10-12 instructions saved if
    factored successfully.

11. **andes_fpexlog.s** - same `__clzsi2` scaffold duplicated twice.
    Similar factoring. Est. ~10 instructions.

12. **andes_dpexexp.s** - two ~24-line save/call-`__gf_sigmax`/restore
    blocks that differ only by the table address (`expcoo` vs `expcon`).
    Factor via `la x10, table; jal .Lcommon` form. Est. ~18-20
    instructions saved. Highest single-file win in this tier.

### Tier D - skip (not worth the risk or effort)

- `andes_dpreduct.s` / `andes_fpreduct.s` - bulk carry-chain code, already
  minimal for ADC-less RISC-V. No clear wins.
- `andes_r.s` / `andes_r4.s` / `andes_n.s` dispatcher bodies - already tight.
- `andes_up4.s` .. `andes_up8.s` - each is a chain of 4 instructions
  tail-calling the next. Cannot shrink without breaking the reuse chain.
- `andes_p*.s` - prologue/epilogue is already minimal; can't eliminate
  saving of the `__adddf3` / `__muldf3` function pointers in x19/x20.
- Structural 4-6x duplication of div/remu/mul loops in `dpexsqrt.s` and
  `fpexatan.s` - large potential but high correctness risk; defer.

## Critical files to modify

Low risk, do first:
- `libm/machine/riscv/andes_0.s`
- `libm/machine/riscv/andes_1.s`
- `libm/machine/riscv/andes_n.s` (verify line 102 first)
- `libm/machine/riscv/andes_fpexsqrt.s`

Medium risk, one file per commit:
- `libm/machine/riscv/andes_fpexatan.s`
- `libm/machine/riscv/andes_dpexsqrt.s`
- `libm/machine/riscv/andes_fpexlog.s`
- `libm/machine/riscv/andes_dpexatan.s`
- `libm/machine/riscv/andes_fpexexp.s`

Higher risk, consider last:
- `libm/machine/riscv/andes_dpexlog.s`
- `libm/machine/riscv/andes_dpexexp.s`

## Pattern references (already-landed commits)

- `c40cb6ed9` s_sqrt.s - dead `mulhu` in squared-norm computation
- `b672cb2f9` s_fmod.s - hoist duplicate `li 0x80000000`
- `026bf3e50` s_log1p.s - `li 0; sw` pairs -> `sw x0`
- `f21da4f44` s_log1p.s - swap `__adddf3` args to eliminate `mv` pair
- `69fe05243` s_expm1.s - redundant loads, li+sw zero folds, li hoists

These are the exact patterns to mirror here.

## Verification protocol (per commit)

Each commit must pass the Makefile A/B compare for every function that
reaches the modified helper. Mapping (from call-graph in CLAUDE.md):

- andes_0.s / andes_1.s: used by j0/j1/y0/y1 - not in the current
  `FUNCS_DOUBLE_*` test list, so verify by building `libandes.a` cleanly
  and comparing `size` before/after. No runtime test available.
- andes_n.s: used by jn/yn - same as above; size check only.
- andes_fpexsqrt.s -> sqrtf, hypotf-path in float: `make test-sqrtf`
- andes_fpexatan.s -> atanf, asinf, acosf: `make test-atanf test-asinf test-acosf`
- andes_fpexlog.s -> logf, log10f, powf: `make test-logf test-log10f test-powf`
- andes_fpexexp.s -> expf, coshf, sinhf, tanhf, powf:
  `make test-expf test-coshf test-sinhf test-tanhf test-powf`
- andes_dpexsqrt.s -> sqrt, hypot-path: `make test-sqrt`
- andes_dpexatan.s -> atan, asin, acos: `make test-atan test-asin test-acos`
- andes_dpexlog.s -> log, log10, pow: `make test-log test-log10 test-pow`
- andes_dpexexp.s -> exp, cosh, sinh, tanh, pow:
  `make test-exp test-cosh test-sinh test-tanh test-pow`

Between each commit:
1. `make clean && make` to rebuild `libandes.a` and all test elfs.
2. `make size-all` and compare TOTAL against baseline captured before edits.
3. `make test-<f>` for every function mapped above. All must report `PASS`.
4. Only then `git commit` using `/commit` skill (commit-optimizer) to
   capture size delta.

## Execution order

1. Capture baseline: `make clean && make size-all > /tmp/andes-size-baseline.txt`
2. Tier A edits, one commit per file, verify after each.
3. Tier B edits (dead muls + constant hoists), one file per commit,
   verify after each. Stop and investigate if any test FAILs - do not
   batch-fix.
4. Tier C (scaffolding refactors) only if Tiers A+B complete cleanly.
   Each refactor gets its own commit and full verification sweep.
5. Final size report: `make size-all > /tmp/andes-size-after.txt` and
   diff against baseline.

## Notes / open questions

- andes_n.s line 102 `addi sp, sp, #8` comment vs `addi sp, sp, 16`
  instruction: confirm whether this is a documented hand-translation
  comment that went stale, or a latent bug. Read git blame first.
- Some duplicate-`li` hoists require finding a free callee-save register;
  if all x9/x18-x21 are live across the duplication span, hoist is not
  possible and that item is dropped.
- Estimated total savings across all tiers (very rough, pre-verification):
  50-90 instructions, i.e. 200-360 bytes of `.text` in the andes helpers.
  Tier A alone is ~20 instructions / ~80 bytes at zero risk.

1. Capture baseline: `make clean && make size-all > /tmp/andes-size-baseline.txt`
2. Tier A edits, one commit per file, verify after each.
3. Tier B edits (dead muls + constant hoists), one file per commit,
   verify after each. Stop and investigate if any test FAILs - do not
   batch-fix.
4. Tier C (scaffolding refactors) only if Tiers A+B complete cleanly.
   Each refactor gets its own commit and full verification sweep.
5. Final size report: `make size-all > /tmp/andes-size-after.txt` and
   diff against baseline.

## Notes / open questions

- andes_n.s line 102 `addi sp, sp, #8` comment vs `addi sp, sp, 16`
  instruction: confirm whether this is a documented hand-translation
  comment that went stale, or a latent bug. Read git blame first.
- Some duplicate-`li` hoists require finding a free callee-save register;
  if all x9/x18-x21 are live across the duplication span, hoist is not
  possible and that item is dropped.

  50-90 instructions, i.e. 200-360 bytes of `.text` in the andes helpers.

input.plan:
# Plan: Andes helper size optimizations

## Context

The RISC-V libm under `libm/machine/riscv/` has ~40 hand-written `andes_*.s`
helper files shared by all higher-level math functions. Prior work has been
focused on the `s_*.s` / `sf_*.s` top-level routines (dead-mul removal,
`sw x0` for zero stores, constant hoisting). The helper files were not yet
audited. This plan is the result of a size audit of the top-level helpers
(not rv32e). The goal is to remove dead code and boilerplate duplication
without touching behavior, verified via the existing `make test-*` A/B
comparison.

## Findings - candidate edits

Ranked by risk (low to higher) and by estimated byte savings.

### Tier A - trivial, mechanical, zero behavioral risk

1. **andes_0.s** - 4x foldable adjacent `addi sp, sp, -16` pairs.
   Each pair (two separate qzero / pzero prologues/epilogues) collapses to a
   single `addi sp, sp, -32` / `+32`. Est. 8 instructions removed.
   File lines ~219-220, ~235-236 (and matching epilogues).

2. **andes_1.s** - identical pattern to andes_0.s. Est. 8 instructions removed.

3. **andes_n.s** - two adjacent `addi sp, sp, 16` at lines ~53-54 and
   ~101-102. Investigate the line 102 `16 vs 8` comment mismatch first -
   may be a latent bug in the hand-translated code or just a stale comment.
   If comment is stale, fold to single `addi sp, sp, 32`. Est. 2
   instructions removed.

4. **andes_fpexsqrt.s** - three apparently-dead `mul` instructions in a
   7-line span (lines 48-49, 51-52, 53-54): each writes to `x14` or `x10`
   that is overwritten before any read. Same dead-`mul` pattern fixed in
   `s_sqrt.s` commit c40cb6ed9. Est. 3 instructions removed in the
   smallest helper.

### Tier B - dead `mul` removals in other helpers

Apply the same pattern used in `s_sqrt.s`: delete `mul` instructions whose
destination is overwritten before use. Each must be individually verified
against surrounding register life by reading a 10-line window and then by
`make test-<func>` for every function that reaches the helper.

5. **andes_fpexatan.s** - ~4 dead muls at lines ~145-146, ~212-213,
   ~228-229, ~240-241. Also 3x duplicate `li x15, 2147483648` across
   lines ~86, 155, 270 (hoistable into a callee-save x-reg spanning the
   whole function body if a free callee-save exists - needs liveness
   verification, else skip).

6. **andes_dpexsqrt.s** - ~4 dead muls in the even/odd exponent arms
   (lines 18-20, 32-34). Plus 4x `li x9, 65535` at lines 55, 97, 116,
   135 - hoist to a single load at function entry if x9 is preserved.

7. **andes_fpexlog.s** - ~2 dead muls (lines ~162-166, ~174-175). Plus
   4x duplicate `li x21, 2147483648` - hoist. Also prologue/epilogue
   pushes sp twice (split pushm pattern); combining into a single frame
   adjust saves 2 instructions.

8. **andes_dpexatan.s** - 1 clearly dead `mul` at line ~175-178.
   Plus multiple duplicate `li ..., 2147483648` loads.

9. **andes_fpexexp.s** - 1 dead `mul` at line ~75-78 (same pattern).

### Tier C - duplicate scaffolding (bigger wins, higher risk)

10. **andes_dpexlog.s** - two identical 14-line push/call `__clzsi2` /
    pop scaffolds. Consider factoring to a local helper block or using
    fall-through to a shared tail. Est. ~10-12 instructions saved if
    factored successfully.

11. **andes_fpexlog.s** - same `__clzsi2` scaffold duplicated twice.
    Similar factoring. Est. ~10 instructions.

12. **andes_dpexexp.s** - two ~24-line save/call-`__gf_sigmax`/restore
    blocks that differ only by the table address (`expcoo` vs `expcon`).
    Factor via `la x10, table; jal .Lcommon` form. Est. ~18-20
    instructions saved. Highest single-file win in this tier.

### Tier D - skip (not worth the risk or effort)

- `andes_dpreduct.s` / `andes_fpreduct.s` - bulk carry-chain code, already
  minimal for ADC-less RISC-V. No clear wins.
- `andes_r.s` / `andes_r4.s` / `andes_n.s` dispatcher bodies - already tight.
- `andes_up4.s` .. `andes_up8.s` - each is a chain of 4 instructions
  tail-calling the next. Cannot shrink without breaking the reuse chain.
- `andes_p*.s` - prologue/epilogue is already minimal; can't eliminate
  saving of the `__adddf3` / `__muldf3` function pointers in x19/x20.
- Structural 4-6x duplication of div/remu/mul loops in `dpexsqrt.s` and
  `fpexatan.s` - large potential but high correctness risk; defer.

## Critical files to modify

Low risk, do first:
- `libm/machine/riscv/andes_0.s`
- `libm/machine/riscv/andes_1.s`
- `libm/machine/riscv/andes_n.s` (verify line 102 first)
- `libm/machine/riscv/andes_fpexsqrt.s`

Medium risk, one file per commit:
- `libm/machine/riscv/andes_fpexatan.s`
- `libm/machine/riscv/andes_dpexsqrt.s`
- `libm/machine/riscv/andes_fpexlog.s`
- `libm/machine/riscv/andes_dpexatan.s`
- `libm/machine/riscv/andes_fpexexp.s`

Higher risk, consider last:
- `libm/machine/riscv/andes_dpexlog.s`
- `libm/machine/riscv/andes_dpexexp.s`

## Pattern references (already-landed commits)

- `c40cb6ed9` s_sqrt.s - dead `mulhu` in squared-norm computation
- `b672cb2f9` s_fmod.s - hoist duplicate `li 0x80000000`
- `026bf3e50` s_log1p.s - `li 0; sw` pairs -> `sw x0`
- `f21da4f44` s_log1p.s - swap `__adddf3` args to eliminate `mv` pair
- `69fe05243` s_expm1.s - redundant loads, li+sw zero folds, li hoists

These are the exact patterns to mirror here.

## Verification protocol (per commit)

Each commit must pass the Makefile A/B compare for every function that
reaches the modified helper. Mapping (from call-graph in CLAUDE.md):

- andes_0.s / andes_1.s: used by j0/j1/y0/y1 - not in the current
  `FUNCS_DOUBLE_*` test list, so verify by building `libandes.a` cleanly
  and comparing `size` before/after. No runtime test available.
- andes_n.s: used by jn/yn - same as above; size check only.
- andes_fpexsqrt.s -> sqrtf, hypotf-path in float: `make test-sqrtf`
- andes_fpexatan.s -> atanf, asinf, acosf: `make test-atanf test-asinf test-acosf`
- andes_fpexlog.s -> logf, log10f, powf: `make test-logf test-log10f test-powf`
- andes_fpexexp.s -> expf, coshf, sinhf, tanhf, powf:
  `make test-expf test-coshf test-sinhf test-tanhf test-powf`
- andes_dpexsqrt.s -> sqrt, hypot-path: `make test-sqrt`
- andes_dpexatan.s -> atan, asin, acos: `make test-atan test-asin test-acos`
- andes_dpexlog.s -> log, log10, pow: `make test-log test-log10 test-pow`
- andes_dpexexp.s -> exp, cosh, sinh, tanh, pow:
  `make test-exp test-cosh test-sinh test-tanh test-pow`

Between each commit:
1. `make clean && make` to rebuild `libandes.a` and all test elfs.
2. `make size-all` and compare TOTAL against baseline captured before edits.
3. `make test-<f>` for every function mapped above. All must report `PASS`.
4. Only then `git commit` using `/commit` skill (commit-optimizer) to
   capture size delta.

## Execution order

1. Capture baseline: `make clean && make size-all > /tmp/andes-size-baseline.txt`
2. Tier A edits, one commit per file, verify after each.
3. Tier B edits (dead muls + constant hoists), one file per commit,
   verify after each. Stop and investigate if any test FAILs - do not
   batch-fix.
4. Tier C (scaffolding refactors) only if Tiers A+B complete cleanly.
   Each refactor gets its own commit and full verification sweep.
5. Final size report: `make size-all > /tmp/andes-size-after.txt` and
   diff against baseline.

## Notes / open questions

- andes_n.s line 102 `addi sp, sp, #8` comment vs `addi sp, sp, 16`
  instruction: confirm whether this is a documented hand-translation
  comment that went stale, or a latent bug. Read git blame first.
- Some duplicate-`li` hoists require finding a free callee-save register;
  if all x9/x18-x21 are live across the duplication span, hoist is not
  possible and that item is dropped.
- Estimated total savings across all tiers (very rough, pre-verification):
  50-90 instructions, i.e. 200-360 bytes of `.text` in the andes helpers.
  Tier A alone is ~20 instructions / ~80 bytes at zero risk.

# Andes helper size optimization plan

Audit of `andes_*.s` (top-level, not rv32e) for size-reducing edits.
Execute one item per commit. After each: `make clean && make && make size-all`
and run the mapped `make test-*` targets. All must PASS before committing.

Patterns mirror prior commits on `s_*.s`:
- c40cb6ed9 dead `mul`/`mulhu` removal
- b672cb2f9 hoist duplicate `li` constants
- 026bf3e50 `li 0; sw` -> `sw x0`
- f21da4f44 swap `__adddf3` args to kill `mv` pair

---

## Tier A - trivial, mechanical (start here)

### [A1] andes_0.s - fold adjacent `addi sp, sp, -16` pairs
- Two pairs in qzero prologue+epilogue (~lines 219-220, matching epilogue)
- Two pairs in pzero prologue+epilogue (~lines 235-236, matching epilogue)
- Each pair collapses to single `addi sp, sp, -32` / `+32`
- Saves ~4 instructions / 16 bytes
- Verify: `make size-all` (no test target covers j0/y0)

### [A2] andes_1.s - same as A1
- Same pattern in qone/pone
- Saves ~4 instructions / 16 bytes
- Verify: `make size-all`

### [A3] andes_n.s - fold adjacent sp adjusts (investigate first)
- Lines ~53-54 and ~101-102 have two adjacent `addi sp, sp, 16`
- Line 102 source comment says `#8` but instruction is `16`. Check git blame
  to confirm whether the comment is stale or there is a latent bug. If bug,
  fix it as separate commit before folding.
- After confirmation, collapse adjacent pairs
- Saves ~2 instructions / 8 bytes
- Verify: `make size-all`

### [A4] andes_fpexsqrt.s - remove 3 dead `mul` instructions
- Lines 48-49: `mulhu x15, x13, x9; mul x14, x13, x9` - x14 killed before read
- Lines 51-52: `mulhu x15, x9, x18; mul x14, x9, x18` - x14 killed before read
- Lines 53-54: `mulhu x11, x15, x10; mul x10, x15, x10` - x10 overwritten
- Verify each dead-ness by 10-line window read before editing
- Saves ~3 instructions / 12 bytes in a 71-line file (largest relative win)
- Verify: `make test-sqrtf`

---

## Tier B - dead mul removals + li hoists (one commit per file)

### [B1] andes_fpexatan.s
- Dead muls at ~145-146, ~212-213, ~228-229, ~240-241 (verify each)
- Duplicate `li x15, 2147483648` at ~86, 155, 270 - hoist if liveness allows
- Saves ~4-7 instructions
- Verify: `make test-atanf test-asinf test-acosf`

### [B2] andes_dpexsqrt.s
- Dead muls in even/odd exponent arms (lines 18-20, 32-34)
- Duplicate `li x9, 65535` at lines 55, 97, 116, 135 - hoist if x9 is free
  across the span
- Saves ~4-7 instructions
- Verify: `make test-sqrt`

### [B3] andes_fpexlog.s
- Dead muls at ~162-166, ~174-175 (verify each)
- Duplicate `li x21, 2147483648` at ~71, 82, 211, 261 - hoist candidate
- Epilogue splits into 32+16-byte pops; combine if safe
- Saves ~4-8 instructions
- Verify: `make test-logf test-log10f test-powf`

### [B4] andes_dpexatan.s
- Dead mul at ~175-178
- Duplicate `li ..., 2147483648` loads - hoist candidates
- Saves ~2-5 instructions
- Verify: `make test-atan test-asin test-acos`

### [B5] andes_fpexexp.s
- Dead mul at ~75-78 (`mul x10, x18, x10` then `mv x10, x11`)
- Saves ~1 instruction
- Verify: `make test-expf test-coshf test-sinhf test-tanhf test-powf`

---

## Tier C - scaffolding factoring (higher risk, do last)

### [C1] andes_dpexlog.s - factor duplicate `__clzsi2` scaffolds
- Two identical 14-line push/call/pop blocks around __clzsi2 (lines ~119-133
  and ~207-221)
- Collapse into a single in-file helper label + two calls
- Saves ~10-12 instructions if done cleanly
- Verify: `make test-log test-log10 test-pow`

### [C2] andes_fpexlog.s - factor duplicate `__clzsi2` scaffolds
- Same pattern as C1 applied to fpexlog
- Saves ~10 instructions
- Verify: `make test-logf test-log10f test-powf`

### [C3] andes_dpexexp.s - factor duplicate `__gf_sigmax` call blocks
- Two ~24-line save+call+restore sequences differing only in table address
  (expcoo vs expcon), lines ~129-152 and ~154-176
- Refactor to load table pointer before a shared `call`/`tail` block
- Saves ~18-20 instructions - largest single-file win
- Verify: `make test-exp test-cosh test-sinh test-tanh test-pow`

---

## Skipped (not worth it)

- andes_dpreduct.s / andes_fpreduct.s - carry-chain code already minimal for
  ADC-less RISC-V
- andes_r.s / andes_r4.s - dispatcher code already tight
- andes_up4.s .. andes_up8.s - each chains to the next; tightest possible
- andes_p4.s .. andes_p8ac.s - prologue/epilogue minimal
- Structural duplication of div/remu loops in dpexsqrt / fpexatan / dpexlog -
  large potential, high correctness risk, defer indefinitely

---

## Verification checklist (per commit)

1. Read 10-line window around each edit to confirm register liveness.
2. `make clean && make` - must build cleanly.
3. `make size-all > /tmp/after.txt` and diff vs baseline to confirm shrink.
4. `make test-<f>` for every mapped function - every one must `PASS`.
5. Commit via `/commit` (commit-optimizer skill) to capture size delta.
6. If any test FAILs: revert, re-read, do not batch-fix.

## Baseline capture (do once before starting)

```
make clean && make
make size-all > /tmp/andes-size-baseline.txt
```

10. **andes_dpexlog.s** - two identical 14-line push/call `__clzsi2` /
    pop scaffolds. Consider factoring to a local helper block or using
    fall-through to a shared tail. Est. ~10-12 instructions saved if
    factored successfully.

11. **andes_fpexlog.s** - same `__clzsi2` scaffold duplicated twice.
    Similar factoring. Est. ~10 instructions.

12. **andes_dpexexp.s** - two ~24-line save/call-`__gf_sigmax`/restore
    blocks that differ only by the table address (`expcoo` vs `expcon`).
    Factor via `la x10, table; jal .Lcommon` form. Est. ~18-20
    instructions saved. Highest single-file win in this tier.

### Tier D - skip (not worth the risk or effort)

- `andes_dpreduct.s` / `andes_fpreduct.s` - bulk carry-chain code, already
  minimal for ADC-less RISC-V. No clear wins.
- `andes_r.s` / `andes_r4.s` / `andes_n.s` dispatcher bodies - already tight.
- `andes_up4.s` .. `andes_up8.s` - each is a chain of 4 instructions
  tail-calling the next. Cannot shrink without breaking the reuse chain.
- `andes_p*.s` - prologue/epilogue is already minimal; can't eliminate
  saving of the `__adddf3` / `__muldf3` function pointers in x19/x20.
- Structural 4-6x duplication of div/remu/mul loops in `dpexsqrt.s` and
  `fpexatan.s` - large potential but high correctness risk; defer.

## Critical files to modify

Low risk, do first:
- `libm/machine/riscv/andes_0.s`
- `libm/machine/riscv/andes_1.s`
- `libm/machine/riscv/andes_n.s` (verify line 102 first)

Medium risk, one file per commit:

- `libm/machine/riscv/andes_fpexlog.s`
- `libm/machine/riscv/andes_dpexatan.s`

Higher risk, consider last:

- `libm/machine/riscv/andes_dpexexp.s`

## Pattern references (already-landed commits)

- `c40cb6ed9` s_sqrt.s - dead `mulhu` in squared-norm computation
- `b672cb2f9` s_fmod.s - hoist duplicate `li 0x80000000`
- `026bf3e50` s_log1p.s - `li 0; sw` pairs -> `sw x0`
- `f21da4f44` s_log1p.s - swap `__adddf3` args to eliminate `mv` pair
- `69fe05243` s_expm1.s - redundant loads, li+sw zero folds, li hoists

These are the exact patterns to mirror here.

## Verification protocol (per commit)

Each commit must pass the Makefile A/B compare for every function that
reaches the modified helper. Mapping (from call-graph in CLAUDE.md):

- andes_0.s / andes_1.s: used by j0/j1/y0/y1 - not in the current
  `FUNCS_DOUBLE_*` test list, so verify by building `libandes.a` cleanly
  and comparing `size` before/after. No runtime test available.
- andes_n.s: used by jn/yn - same as above; size check only.
- andes_fpexsqrt.s -> sqrtf, hypotf-path in float: `make test-sqrtf`
- andes_fpexatan.s -> atanf, asinf, acosf: `make test-atanf test-asinf test-acosf`
- andes_fpexlog.s -> logf, log10f, powf: `make test-logf test-log10f test-powf`
- andes_fpexexp.s -> expf, coshf, sinhf, tanhf, powf:
  `make test-expf test-coshf test-sinhf test-tanhf test-powf`
- andes_dpexsqrt.s -> sqrt, hypot-path: `make test-sqrt`
- andes_dpexatan.s -> atan, asin, acos: `make test-atan test-asin test-acos`
- andes_dpexlog.s -> log, log10, pow: `make test-log test-log10 test-pow`
- andes_dpexexp.s -> exp, cosh, sinh, tanh, pow:
  `make test-exp test-cosh test-sinh test-tanh test-pow`

Between each commit:
1. `make clean && make` to rebuild `libandes.a` and all test elfs.
2. `make size-all` and compare TOTAL against baseline captured before edits.
3. `make test-<f>` for every function mapped above. All must report `PASS`.
4. Only then `git commit` using `/commit` skill (commit-optimizer) to
   capture size delta.

## Execution order

1. Capture baseline: `make clean && make size-all > /tmp/andes-size-baseline.txt`
2. Tier A edits, one commit per file, verify after each.
3. Tier B edits (dead muls + constant hoists), one file per commit,
   verify after each. Stop and investigate if any test FAILs - do not
   batch-fix.
4. Tier C (scaffolding refactors) only if Tiers A+B complete cleanly.
   Each refactor gets its own commit and full verification sweep.
5. Final size report: `make size-all > /tmp/andes-size-after.txt` and
   diff against baseline.

## Notes / open questions

- andes_n.s line 102 `addi sp, sp, #8` comment vs `addi sp, sp, 16`
  instruction: confirm whether this is a documented hand-translation
  comment that went stale, or a latent bug. Read git blame first.
- Some duplicate-`li` hoists require finding a free callee-save register;
  if all x9/x18-x21 are live across the duplication span, hoist is not
  possible and that item is dropped.
- Estimated total savings across all tiers (very rough, pre-verification):
  50-90 instructions, i.e. 200-360 bytes of `.text` in the andes helpers.
  Tier A alone is ~20 instructions / ~80 bytes at zero risk.
